steps.title: Spot the decisive evidence
steps.content: The passage shows a stark gap: countries open to foreign-capital inflows had a 0.20 probability of receiving swap lines, versus only 0.03 for countries with restrictive policies. The blank describes how a country's policy environment relates to whether it received a swap line.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
The blank needs to mean important for determining swap-line allocation. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — material to: significant or relevant to — the 0.20 vs. 0.03 gap shows policy environment was directly significant to swap-line outcomes. ✓
  • Choice B — predicated on: based entirely upon — this overstates the relationship; policy environment influenced but didn't solely determine outcomes. ✗
  • Choice C — decoupled from: separated from or unrelated to — the large probability gap proves the opposite: policy and swap lines were linked. ✗
  • Choice D — mediated by: controlled or transmitted through an intermediary — this suggests a third factor bridges policy and swap lines, which the passage doesn't claim. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
